name: research_ethics_ransomware_incident_forces_lumen_jus_mer
premise: A Research Ethics response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving research ethics ransomware incident forces lumen jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Camila Nowak
agents.identity: I am Camila Nowak, accountable for a defensible call in Research Ethics when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Kaito Kim
agents.identity: I am Kaito Kim, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Research Ethics.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Camila Nowak must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Camila Nowak do?
